summary: chalk-plus-ts postinstall fetches and executes remote code from jsonbin.io
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook `node lib/utils/index.js` that spawns a detached child process running lib/utils/smtp-connection/index.js. That file uses obfuscator.io-style string-array + hex-indexed dispatcher obfuscation (identifiers like `_0x926305`, `_0x55ca`, `_0x438c`) to hide a fetch to https://api.jsonbin.io/v3/b/6a461991f5f4af5e295272d4 and then executes the returned `record.cookie` field via `new Function('require', <remote_code>)(require)`. This gives the publisher install-time arbitrary code execution on any machine that runs `npm install chalk-plus-ts`, and because the payload is fetched from a mutable jsonbin.io record the delivered code can be changed at any time. Detached spawn allows the child to continue running after npm exits. The package additionally impersonates nodemailer (ships nodemailer's source, uses author `Andris Reinman`) under an unrelated chalk-themed name with a mismatched React Training description, which is a lure to attract installs from developers looking for chalk or nodemailer utilities.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
